target_statement: Determine the optimal constant μ such that lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) = μ under RH

propositions.statement: RH: All non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) lie on the critical line Re(s) = 1/2, so ρ_n = 1/2 + iγ_n
Montgomery's pair correlation conjecture: normalized gaps of zeta zeros follow GUE statistics for |α| < 1
Under RH, current best rigorous upper bound: lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) < 0.50895
The GUE nearest-neighbor spacing distribution is S(s) = (π/2)s·exp(-πs²/4)
GUE joint probability density is proportional to product of |λ_i - λ_j| times exponential potential, encoding level repulsion
Zeros γ_n can be computed to arbitrary precision up to n = 10^13 using Odlyzko-Schönhage algorithm
Define normalized gap d_n = (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π), which is the natural local scaling for gap statistics
The empirical gap distribution from computed zeros up to γ_n ≈ 10^13 can be compared against GUE spacing distribution S(s)
The integral I = (1/(2√π)) ∫₀^∞ s·S(s)·e^(-s) ds can be numerically evaluated to arbitrary precision
Define k-point gap correlation function G_k(s_1,...,s_k;T) measuring joint distribution of k consecutive normalized gaps among zeros with imaginary parts up to T
GUE model predicts polynomial rigidity: probability of k consecutive gaps all smaller than ε decays like ε^(k(k+1)/2)
Empirical rigidity length scale ℓ_rigid(T) can be measured from computed zeros by analyzing suppression of fluctuations at various scales
G_k factorizes under RH and Montgomery's conjecture with correction terms encoding higher-order rigidity: G_k ≈ ∏ S(s_i) · C_k(s_1,...,s_k) where C_k captures k-body correlations
Pigeonhole-energy argument: partition zeros into windows and bound total gap energy ∑ d_n in each window using GUE rigidity to derive μ ≥ c·(1-δ(W)) as W→∞
The optimal constant satisfies μ ≥ (1/(2√π)) ∫₀^∞ s·S(s)·e^(-s) ds by treating minimal gap as minimum of modulated Poisson process with GUE spacing statistics
There exists a rigidity length scale ℓ_rigid(∞) = lim_{T→∞} ℓ_rigid(T) such that μ ~ 1/ℓ_rigid(∞), linking minimal spacing to global rigidity property
If the GUE model accurately describes zero gap statistics and rigidity properties hold as predicted, then μ should be bounded below by a constant determined by GUE extreme value theory

Under RH, the problem of determining optimal μ in lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n)/(2π) = μ remains open, with rigorous bound μ < 0.50895, GUE heuristic suggesting μ ≈ 0.318, and several conjectural mechanisms proposed but unproven

chain.statement: Assume RH and establish normalized gap framework: under RH all zeros lie on Re(s)=1/2, and the natural normalized gap is d_n = (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n)/(2π)
Define k-point correlation functions G_k to study joint gap distributions, generalizing Montgomery's pair correlation to higher order statistics
Use computed zeros up to 10^13 to empirically verify that gap distribution closely matches GUE spacing distribution S(s)
Numerically evaluate the integral I = (1/(2√π)) ∫₀^∞ s·S(s)·e^(-s) ds using the GUE spacing formula
Extract empirical rigidity length scale from computed zeros by measuring fluctuation suppression at various window sizes
From GUE repulsion mechanism and polynomial rigidity prediction, infer that minimal gaps should be controlled by extreme value statistics in a rigid point process
RISKY: Conjecture that G_k factorizes with correction terms under extended Montgomery conjecture
RISKY: Apply pigeonhole-energy argument with window partitioning to derive lower bound on μ using GUE rigidity
RISKY: Propose explicit integral formula for lower bound by treating minimal gap as extreme value of modulated Poisson process
RISKY: Conjecture connection between minimal spacing constant μ and asymptotic rigidity length scale
Synthesize current state of the problem: rigorous bound μ < 0.50895 is established; GUE heuristics and numerical evidence suggest μ ≈ 0.318; several conjectural mechanisms are proposed but unproven

summary: The optimal constant μ remains undetermined: rigorous upper bound 0.50895 exists, GUE heuristics suggest μ ≈ 0.318 via integral formula, and experiments can test gap statistics against GUE predictions. Four major conjectural mechanisms lack rigorous justification, representing the current frontier of this open problem.
